title: rosa pomifera var. gibellii
intro: Rosa pomifera var. gibellii, also known as the wild rose or prairie rose, is a species of rose native to North America, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible hips. The medicinal properties of Rosa pomifera var. gibellii lie in its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of consuming Rosa pomifera var. gibellii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, as well as providing relief from menstrual cramps and menopausal symptoms. Rosa pomifera var. gibellii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as used in salves and ointments for topical applications.
